title: Pogonospermum callothamnum
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Pogonospermum callothamnum. However, I think you may be referring to Pogostemon cablin, also known as Pogostemon callosus or Patchouli.

Pogostemon cablin is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as patchouli. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of patchouli include patchoulol, pogostone, and other sesquiterpene compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, patchouli is often used in topical preparations, such as creams, ointments, and essential oils, as well as in teas and tinctures, to leverage its therapeutic effects.
